citation_short: CNMH, Bojayá: la guerra sin límites (2010)
title: Bojayá: la guerra sin límites
strengths: Best single source for separating the 79 direct church deaths from broader and later mortality, while also giving a usable site-specific shelter count.
limitations: Does not supply a clean combatant-victim denominator suitable for formal DR.

title: Colombia: Bojaya Civilians Trapped by Fighting
strengths: Captures the high early casualty estimates circulating immediately after the event.
limitations: Early crisis estimate; not as stable as later CNMH reconstruction.
notes: Used only to show why the bundle privileges the later, better-separated CNMH direct-death count.

citation_short: CNMH, Bojayá: La guerra sin límites
title: Bojayá: La guerra sin límites
strengths: Load-bearing narrative reconstruction with an explicit fatality partition for the church massacre and contextual figures on how many civilians sheltered in the church.
limitations: Does not provide a sex-age composition for the 300 people sheltering inside the church, so event-specific women/children person-month denominators cannot be recovered.
notes: CNMH states that 79 people died in the church, 48 of them minors; 41 females were killed in total including girls; among the 29 adults, 16 were women and 13 were men.

title: Censo General 2005 - Perfil municipio Bojayá (27099)
pages_or_sections: Municipal profile summary page and age-sex chart
strengths: Official local demographic baseline with a sex split and age-sex bands that allow a transparent under-18 approximation.
limitations: Postdates the massacre by three years and describes the municipality rather than the church refuge population.
notes: The profile gives female share 48.5%, male share 51.5%, ages 0-14 equal 49.4% in total, and age 15-19 split by sex, allowing a 60% allocation to approximate ages 15-17.
